job_location: Billund, Denmark or Boston, Massachusetts, United States

evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ensure Data Products follow CI/CD standards, adhere to data quality frameworks; include assertion checks and have performance & cost optimization applied.
evidence.collaboration_width: Analytics Interface; Commercial Analytics; Analytics Innovation & Automation; Data Office; Shopper & Partner (D2C & B2B) digital product teams; Data Science; Marketing; Finance; Operations
evidence.data_team_maturity: Our Commercial A&I function is recognized by the executive leadership team for spearheading the growth in analytics maturity at the LEGO Group, and driving quantifiable commercial value through data and insights!
evidence.jd_authorship: Build and maintain semantic layer infrastructure including metric view pipelines, materialization and optimization; Drive Unity Catalog governance (schemas, access, metadata tagging)
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: enable self-service data products for Markets & Channels (Commercial) data consumers and decision makers
evidence.autonomy_level: Build data pipeline engineering, orchestration, and monitoring to deliver high-quality data products centered around Retail Execution Commercial pillar; Drive Unity Catalog governance
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build data pipeline engineering, orchestration, and monitoring to deliver high-quality data products; Partner with the Analytics Innovation & Automation and Data Office product teams to prototype & deliver innovative features across the Data Platform
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The quote blends building new pipeline infrastructure with partnering on innovative product features, reflecting a mix of net-new build and ongoing platform delivery.
evidence.work_arrangement: Hybrid working model: 3 days in office per week.
evidence.ai_role: semantic layer infrastructure, Analytics Innovation & Automation
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD mentions an 'AI-enabled data foundation' and 'AI-driven insights' as outputs of the role's infrastructure work but never asks the candidate to use AI coding tools themselves.
evidence.testing_framing: Ensure Data Products follow CI/CD standards, adhere to data quality frameworks; include assertion checks and have performance & cost optimization applied.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: The verb 'Ensure' assigns the candidate direct ownership of data quality frameworks and assertion checks rather than merely listing testing tools.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Compliance is named directly as the operating context for governance work, but it sits alongside commercial growth and analytics maturity framing rather than dominating the JD, supporting a moderate-leaning-high read; the existing 'high' label centers on this explicit compliance phrase.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Multiple rigour signals emphasizing data quality frameworks, CI/CD standards, governance, compliance, and best practices; only one velocity signal appears in nice-to-have section.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: LEGO is a retail/consumer goods company; data errors affect commercial decisions but lack financial reporting, regulatory, or healthcare implications triggering high risk.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The function is established and recognized at executive level with multiple named sub-teams, existing platforms (Databricks), and governance structures (Unity Catalog) already in place.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical terms (semantic layer, metric views, materialization, Unity Catalog, CI/CD, assertion checks) indicate domain expertise, but these are mixed throughout with generic collaboration boilerplate typical of recruiter involvement.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role's primary purpose is to build data platforms and infrastructure (semantic layers, governance, pipelines) for self-serve consumption by data consumers and platform users.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The responsibilities emphasize strategic ownership verbs (build, drive, enable, champion) for defining and shaping data platform direction, rather than executing someone else's priorities.
